title: Understanding Warning-WIDTHEXPAND
body:
Hello,

I am working on a student project and I use Verilator (which I find amazing, BTW).
I am having trouble understanding a warning that is output by `verilator --lint-only`.

I have the following Verilog module in a file `tmp.sv`:
```
module tmp(input [2:0] a, b, c, output [3:0] d);
  assign d = a + (b & c);
endmodule
```

If I call `verilator --lint-only tmp.sv`, I obtain the following warning:
```
%Warning-WIDTHEXPAND: tmp.sv:2:21: Operator AND expects 4 bits on the LHS, but LHS's VARREF 'b' generates 3 bits.
                                 : ... note: In instance 'tmp'
    2 |   assign d = a + (b & c);
      |                     ^
                      ... For warning description see https://verilator.org/warn/WIDTHEXPAND?v=5.043
                      ... Use "/* verilator lint_off WIDTHEXPAND */" and lint_on around source to disable this message.
%Warning-WIDTHEXPAND: tmp.sv:2:21: Operator AND expects 4 bits on the RHS, but RHS's VARREF 'c' generates 3 bits.
                                 : ... note: In instance 'tmp'
    2 |   assign d = a + (b & c);
      |                     ^
%Error: Exiting due to 2 warning(s)
```

I do not understand why my signals `b` and `c` are expected to be 4 bits long: the result of the AND operation is added to a 3-bit vector.
What do I do wrong?

Thank you for your help,

Ps: I obtain the same warnings if I replace the ADD operator with a subtraction or if I replace the AND operator with a division, modulus, or, xor.
code_blocks:
module tmp(input [2:0] a, b, c, output [3:0] d);
  assign d = a + (b & c);
endmodule
%Warning-WIDTHEXPAND: tmp.sv:2:21: Operator AND expects 4 bits on the LHS, but LHS's VARREF 'b' generates 3 bits.
                                 : ... note: In instance 'tmp'
    2 |   assign d = a + (b & c);
      |                     ^
                      ... For warning description see https://verilator.org/warn/WIDTHEXPAND?v=5.043
                      ... Use "/* verilator lint_off WIDTHEXPAND */" and lint_on around source to disable this message.
%Warning-WIDTHEXPAND: tmp.sv:2:21: Operator AND expects 4 bits on the RHS, but RHS's VARREF 'c' generates 3 bits.
                                 : ... note: In instance 'tmp'
    2 |   assign d = a + (b & c);
      |                     ^
%Error: Exiting due to 2 warning(s)
